text.title: LB Monitoring Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for LB monitoring + observability. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every HAProxy / Nginx LB MUST expose Prometheus metrics via a dedicated exporter sidecar (`haproxy-exporter:9101` against the stats socket, `nginx-prometheus-exporter:9113` against `stub_status`). Plain log-based monitoring is forbidden as the sole source.
rule.rationale: Logs lack the cardinality and aggregation needed for SLO math. Exporters give pull-based, dimensional metrics that scale to thousands of backends.

rule.statement: The alert ruleset MUST include all four: BackendDown (`==0` for 1 m), HighErrorRate (`5xx rate / total > 0.05` for 5 m), HighLatency (p99 > 2 s for 5 m), ConnectionPoolExhausted (`current_conns / maxconn > 0.9` for 2 m). Sub-1-minute `for:` durations are forbidden.
rule.rationale: These four cover the primary user-visible failure modes. Sub-minute durations cause storms on transient blips.

rule.statement: All metrics and alerts MUST carry a `backend` label so dashboards and alerts can attribute failure to a specific backend pool. Aggregate-only metrics (no backend dimension) are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without the dimension, an alert "5xx rate high" cannot tell SRE which backend to look at; MTTR doubles.

rule.statement: LB access logs MUST be shipped to a centralised store (Loki / Elasticsearch / S3) with ≥ 30-day retention. The shipping config MUST parse access-log fields (status, upstream_response_time, request_id) into structured labels.
rule.rationale: Audit + post-incident correlation requires structured access logs. Disk-only logs are wiped on host failure and unsearchable.

rule.statement: Grafana dashboards MUST be templated by `instance` + `backend` Prometheus labels so the same dashboard works across all LBs. Hard-coded backend / instance names are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Templated dashboards scale; bespoke dashboards per LB stop being maintained within a quarter.
